body_txt: A Staged Pedestrian Accident is a scam in which the hustler arranges to be struck by a car, then feigns injuries. A skilled con artist can take hits up to 25 miles per hour, go rattling across the roof and fall into a miserable pile behind the car without even getting a bruise. note Although, of course, actual injury is fairly commonplace since it's impossible to adjust for all variables . This con pays out in a number of different ways. The driver's insurance company will pay out for medical care from a doctor who the hustler is in league with for a split. In countries that have disability payments, that can be milked. If there is a "friendly" lawyer available to work a lawsuit up for "pain and suffering", it can be a very big payout. The hustler can sometimes get an immediate cash settlement from the driver, who may fear that his insurance rates will go up, or who has other reasons not to involve the authorities in the matter. A preferred place to stage this play is near the exit from a bar's parking lot, where the driver will want to avoid a breath test if the police are brought in. A specific example of Obfuscating Disability and Wounded Gazelle Gambit . May overlap with contortionist , where the idea of "rag dolling" comes from. See Frivolous Lawsuit for minor but real accidents (and Ambulance Chaser for the lawyer who goes looking for them and might even aid a con artist). Not to be confused with Make It Look Like an Accident , which is an inversion of this trope, though not always involving a vehicle. Note: This trope used to be called "Flopsy" on this wiki, but it was renamed since the old name was too confusing.
